DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF KENNETH GREEN, JR.'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT IGMIDIO VINO'S FURTHER ANSWERS TO REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS AND FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. THE NON-VERIFIED RESPONSE PREPARED BY COUNSEL IS EQUIVALENT OF NO RESPONSE AT ALL. (APPLETON V. SUPERIOR COURT 206 CAL.APP.3D 632 (1988). THE DEFENDANT SHALL SERVE RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, WITHIN TWENTY-FIVE DAYS OF SERVICE OF A COPY OF THIS ORDER WITH NOTICE OF ENTRY. THE DEFENDANT SHALL BE ADVISED IN AN ORDER PREPARED BY HIS COUNSEL THAT FURTHER FAILURE TO RESPOND MAY SUBJECT HIM TO EVIDENTIARY, ISSUE AND/OR TERMINATING SANCTIONS. COPIES OF THE COMMUNICATIONS BETWEEN THE DEFENDANT AND HIS COUNSEL SUPPORT THE INFERENCE THE FAILURE TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY MAY BE A WILLFUL DISREGARD OF DISCOVERY RESPONSE OBLIGATIONS. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED TO PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $660.00 TO BE PAID BY THE DEFENDANT ONLY WITHIN THE AFOREMENTIONED TWENTY-FIVE DAYS. MOVING PARTY SHALL REVISE AND RE-SUBMIT THE PROPOSED ORDER TO CONFORM WITH THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN B. STEIN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302)

THE NON-VERIFIED RESPONSE SERVED BY COUNSEL IS EQUIVALENT OF NO RESPONSE AT ALL. SEE: APPLETON V. SUPERIOR COURT 206 CAL.APP.3D 632 (1988). THE DEFENDANT SHALL SERVE RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, WITHIN TWENTY-FIVE DAYS OF SERVICE OF A COPY OF THIS ORDER WITH NOTICE OF ENTRY. THE DEFENDANT SHALL BE ADVISED IN AN ORDER PREPARED BY HIS COUNSEL THAT FURTHER FAILURE TO RESPOND MAY SUBJECT HIM TO EVIDENTIARY, ISSUE AND/OR TERMINATING SANCTIONS. COPIES OF THE COMMUNICATIONS BETWEEN THE DEFENDANT AND HIS COUNSEL SUPPORT THE INFERENCE THE FAILURE TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY MAY BE A WILLFUL DISREGARD OF DISCOVERY RESPONSE OBLIGATIONS. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED TO PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $660.00 TO BE PAID BY THE DEFENDANT ONLY WITHIN THE AFOREMENTIONED TWENTY-FIVE DAYS. MOVING PARTY SHALL REVISE AND RE-SUBMIT THE PROPOSED ORDER TO CONFORM WITH THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN B. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Igmidio Vino Further Answers To Special And Form Interrogatories, Set One; And For Sanctions; Memorandum In Support; And Declaration Of Nicholas Musgrove
Off Calendar Oct-03-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nicholas Musgrove, Esq. of the Law Office of Sean Musgrove, Tel. 916.965.4577, for plaintiff and moving party, by court call;
No other appearance.

The tentative ruling was not contested, therefore the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF KENNETH GREEN, JR.'s Motion To Compel Defendant Igmidio Vino's Further Answers To Special And Form Interrogatories, Set One, And For Sanctions as follows: Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to Special And Form Interrogatories, Set One by defendant Vino, is granted. The non-verified response served by counsel is equivalent of no response at all. See: Appleton v. Superior Court 206 Cal.App.3d 632 (1988). The defendant shall serve responses, without objection, within twenty-five days of service of a copy of this Order with Notice of Entry. The defendant shall be advised in an Order prepared by his counsel that further failure to respond may subject him to evidentiary, issue and/or terminating sanctions. Copies of the communications between the defendant and his counsel support the inference the failure to respond to discovery may be a willful disregard of discovery response obligations. Monetary sanctions are awarded to plaintiff in the amount of $660.00 to be paid by the defendant only within the aforementioned twenty-five days. Moving party shall revise and re-submit the proposed order to conform with the substantive portion of the court's tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge Pro Tem: Steven B. Stein; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
